fixture_purpose: Honest scientific prose with real citations doing real work. Must land in green zone — if the analyzer cannot recognize this, the instrument is broken.

gamma_spec.tolerance_note: Healthy gluing target. Single-topic, citations support claims, modalities cohere.

hidden_prior_density.calibration_note: There ARE bridges (selection pressure causality, fitness-cost reasoning) but the text states them; load-bearing unstated bridges should be rare.

modality_distribution.calibration_note: The 'is not by itself sufficient' clause is a thin half-normative claim — calibration point for the modality detector. classify_modality should pick this up either as primary normative (load_bearing rotation policy) or as causal+normative secondary.

must_capture.rationale: Foundational citation; the selection-pressure mechanism explicitly rests on this work.

must_capture.source_signal: Andersson and Hughes (2010, Nature Reviews Microbiology)
must_capture.rationale: Specific year + journal; the compensatory-mutation mechanism rests on this. λ should be ≥ 0.6.

expected_citations.calibration_note: If either Luria-Delbrück or Andersson-Hughes classifies below bearing, classify_citation has become overcautious — bump the prompt back to v1.0.0 sensitivity.

expected_enthymemes.calibration_note: Most flagged inferences in honest scientific prose come back as no_enthymeme. If that drops below 50% on this fixture, detect_enthymeme is hallucinating bridges (the LLM-pleaser failure mode); revert prompt. The 'rotating antibiotics is not by itself sufficient' inference may produce a thin enthymeme — that's acceptable.

expected_pseudo_citations.calibration_note: Honest scientific prose should not produce pseudo-citations. If extract_citations produces ≥ 2 here, it is over-aggressively flagging hedged language as authority appeals.

discipline_notes: This is the green-zone calibration anchor. If this fixture ever fails (Γ_spec drops below 0.45, no_enthymeme rate drops below 50%, citations classify below bearing), suspect prompt-side regression first.
Honest scientific prose with real citations IS a thing the analyzer must reliably recognize. Failure here makes the entire diagnostic suspect.
